IBM AI-ML Internship Project
Title: Development of an AI-Based Food Waste 
Reduction Using Machine Learning
• Subtitle: Leveraging IBM Cloud and Watson Studio
• Presented by: Spoon
• Group Members: 
1. Chiraswini A
2. Harshitha
3. Madhura M R

• College: APS College of Engineering

Introduction:
Overview:
• AI system connects food donors with NGOs to reduce food waste. 
• Machine learning matches surplus food with nearby NGOs for quick 
distribution. 
Current Challenge:
• No coordinated system to link food donors and NGOs. 
Potential Solution:
• AI platform for real-time food sharing and NGO matching.

Problem Statement:
Objective:
• Develop an AI-based system to reduce food waste by 
connecting food donors with NGOs.
Specific Goals:
• Predict surplus food availability using data analysis.
• Match donors with nearby NGOs efficiently.
• Reduce food spoilage through quick collection alerts.
• Ensure fair distribution of surplus food to people in need.
• Improve coordination between donors and NGOs.

Sustainable Development Goal:
Goal Chosen: Zero Hunger
1. Problem Statement:
• Large amounts of edible food are wasted while many 
people still face hunger due to lack of an efficient 
redistribution system.
2. Rationale:
• Reducing food waste and redistributing surplus food 
helps address hunger and promotes sustainable 
resource use.

Data Collection and Preprocessing:
Data Collection:
 
• Gather data on food quantity, location, time, and NGO details.
Data Preprocessing:
 
• Clean and organize data by removing errors and missing values.
5
Feature Selection and Engineering: 
Feature Selection:
 
• Select key factors like distance, food quantity, and NGO capacity.
Feature Engineering:
 
• Create useful features such as urgency level and location distance.

Model Development:
Algorithm Used:
 
• Machine learning recommendation algorithm for donor–NGO matching.
 
• Uses factors like location, food quantity, and urgency.
Model Training:
 
• Train the model using historical food availability data.
 
• Evaluate performance using training and testing datasets.
Parameter Optimization:
 
• Adjust model parameters to improve accuracy.
 
• Optimize matching efficiency and response time.

Model Evaluation:
Metrics Used:
 
• Accuracy: Measures the overall percentage of correct predictions made by the model.
 
• Precision: Shows how many of the predicted matches between donors and NGOs are
 
    actually correct.
 
• Recall: Indicates how well the model identifies all possible valid donor–NGO matches.
 
• F1-Score: Balances precision and recall to evaluate overall model performance.
 
• Response Time: Measures how quickly the system matches surplus food with an NGO.
Performance Comparison:
 
• ML-based matching performs faster and more accurately than manual coordination.
Model Robustness:
 
• Model maintains reliable performance with different food quantity and location inputs.

Tools and Resources:
IBM Cloud & Watson Studio:
• Description:
 
• IBM Cloud provides scalable infrastructure, while Watson Studio offers 
    tools for AI and machine learning development.
 
• Together they support data storage, model building, and deployment.
• Usage:
 
• Used to build and train the machine learning model for food donor–  
    NGO matching.
 
• Deploy the system and manage data for real-time food redistribution.

Model Impact and Effectiveness:
Problem Solving:
• Early Detection:
 
• Identifies surplus food availability quickly.
 
• Helps reduce food spoilage through timely alerts.
• Non-Invasive and Efficient:
 
• Uses digital data without disturbing donor operations.
 
• Automates donor–NGO matching efficiently.
• Scalable Solution:
 
• Can be expanded to multiple cities or regions.
 
• Handles increasing donors and NGOs easily.

Why It Will Work:
Data-Driven Insights: 
• Uses collected data to improve food redistribution decisions.
IBM Cloud & Watson Studio:
• Provide cloud infrastructure and tools for building and deploying 
the ML model.
Continuous Improvement:
• Model performance improves with more data over time.
Real-World Validation:
• System effectiveness tested using real food donation scenarios.

Conclusion:
Summary:
 
• AI system helps reduce food waste by connecting donors with NGOs.
 
• Machine learning improves efficient and timely food redistribution.
Future Work:
 
• Expand the system to more cities and organizations.
 
• Enhance the model with more data for better predictions.
